zh.Salisbury, Wiltshire, England: 英國維爾爾索爾茲伯里
zh.Haverhill, Suffolk, England: 英國素福爾可哈維爾爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Edward Turner於1370年出生在英國艾可哈維爾爾時，他的父親Edward Roger Myer Turner26歲，母親Christina De Chalfhunte21歲。他於1390年與Margaret Mary de Peverell在英國素福爾可結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1431年在英國素福爾可哈維爾爾享年61歲。

zh-hans.Salisbury, Wiltshire, England: 英国維爾爾索爾茲伯里
zh-hans.Haverhill, Suffolk, England: 英国素福爾可哈維爾爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Edward Turner于1370年出生在英国艾可哈維爾爾时，他的父亲Edward Roger Myer Turner26岁，母亲Christina De Chalfhunte21岁。他于1390年与Margaret Mary de Peverell在英国素福爾可结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1431年在英国素福爾可哈維爾爾享年61岁。

nameMeanings.name: Turner
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English t(o)urnour, turner ‘turner’ (Old French to(u)rn(e)our), mainly denoting someone who fashioned small objects of wood, metal, or bone on a lathe, but also a variety of other occupations, including turnspit and translator or interpreter. This surname may have become confused with Toner . In North America, it is also very common among African Americans.

English: occasionally perhaps a nickname from Middle English turn-hare, a compound of Middle English tournen ‘to turn, direct, steer’ + hare ‘hare’, a name for someone in charge of the greyhounds in hare coursing or an exaggerated compliment for someone who could run fast. See also Turnbull .
English: perhaps also from Middle English t(o)urn(e)our ‘jouster, one who takes part in a tournament’ (Old French tornoieor, tournoieur).

variants.name: Trinder
meanings: English (Oxfordshire and Gloucestershire): occupational name from a derivative of Middle English trenden ‘to turn, roll, twist’ (Old English trendan), denoting someone who turned something such as wood or fibres. Compare Turner , Spinner .

variants.name: Toner
meanings: Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Tomhrair ‘descendant of Tomhrar’, a Gaelicized form of the Norse personal name Thorr. Compare Thunder .
English: nickname from Old French toner ‘thunder’, perhaps for someone with a thunderous voice. Compare Thunder .
English: variant of   Towner  .

variants.name: Tarner
meanings: English: variant of   Turner  . This form of the surname is relatively rare in Britain and Ireland. It may sometimes have been confused with   Taverner  .
German (North Rhine-Westphalia): unexplained. This surname is rare in Germany.

variants.name: Turney
meanings: English (of Norman origin):
habitational name from any of the places in northern France called Tournai-sur-Dive (Orne), Tournay-sur-Odon (Calvados), or Tourny (Eure), or occasionally from Tournai in Hainaut (Belgium).

perhaps occasionally an occupational name from Middle English tournei, turnai ‘lawyer’ (Anglo-Norman French tourné), or a nickname from Middle English tournei(en) ‘(to) joust, take part in a tournament’ (Old French torneier). Compare Turner .

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

variants.name: Edoardo
meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Eduardo
meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Eideard
meanings: Gaelic equivalent of Edward .

variants.name: Édouard
meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Edvard
meanings: form of English Edward .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Edward Turner was born in 1370, in Haverhill, Essex, England, his father, Edward Roger Myer Turner, was 26 and his mother, Christina De Chalfhunte, was 21. He married Margaret Mary de Peverell in 1390, in Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died in 1431, in Haverhill, Suffolk, England, at the age of 61.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Edward Turner est né en 1370, à Haverhill, Essex, Angleterre, son père, Edward Roger Myer Turner, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Christina De Chalfhunte, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Margaret Mary de Peverell en 1390, à Suffolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1431, à Haverhill, Suffolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 61 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Edward Turner 1370 in Haverhill, Essex, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Edward Roger Myer Turner 26 und seine Mutter Christina De Chalfhunte 21. Er hat 1390 in Suffolk, England Margaret Mary de Peverell geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1431 im Alter von 61 Jahren in Haverhill, Suffolk, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Edward Turner è nato il nel 1370 nel Haverhill, Essex, England, il suo padre, Edward Roger Myer Turner, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Christina De Chalfhunte, aveva 21 anni. Lui sposato Margaret Mary de Peverell nel 1390, nel Suffolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1431, nel Haverhill, Suffolk, England, all'età di 61 anni.

ja.Haverhill, Essex, England: イギリスエセックスヘイバーヒル

ja.lifeSummary: Edward Turnerが1370年にイギリスエセックスヘイバーヒルで生まれた時，父Edward Roger Myer Turnerは26歳，母Christina De Chalfhunteは21歳であった。1390年にイギリススフォルクでMargaret Mary de Peverellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1431年にイギリススフォルクヘイバーヒルで61歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Edward Turner(이)가 1370년 영국 에세스 하버힐에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Edward Roger Myer Turner(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Christina De Chalfhunte(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1390년 영국 수폴크에서 Margaret Mary de Peverell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1431년 영국 수폴크 하버힐에서 61세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Edward Turner nasceu em 1370, em Haverhill, Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Edward Roger Myer Turner, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Christina De Chalfhunte, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Margaret Mary de Peverell em 1390, em Suffolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1431, em Haverhill, Suffolk, Inglaterra, com 61 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Edward Turner родился в 1370 г. в местности хавирилл, эссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Edward Roger Myer Turner было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Christina De Chalfhunte, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Mary de Peverell в 1390 г. в местности суффолк, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1431 г. в местности хавирилл, суффолк, Англия в возрасте 61 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Edward Turner nació en 1370, en Haverhill, Essex, Inglaterra, su papá, Edward Roger Myer Turner, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Christina De Chalfhunte, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Margaret Mary de Peverell en 1390, en Suffolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió en 1431, en Haverhill, Suffolk, Inglaterra, a la edad de 61 años.
